subject: Re: M to Relion5
body:
If you used a fine deformation model to get it to 4 A, I'd recommend making 
the grid resolution coarser and doing a couple more iterations in M before 
you export particles for classification. Otherwise, particles that don't 
match the average may be overfitted on the average, affecting 
classification performance.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Thursday, June 26, 2025 at 6:35:02 AM UTC-7 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Morning Mart,
>
> 4A, nice!
>
> Sorry, export directly from M is in the long tail of features that hasn’t 
> been finalized in M - you’ll have to write a script that modifies your M 
> particle star file making it compatible with WarpTools particle export, 
> details here https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/149
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Jun 26, 2025, at 06:03, Mart Last <mgf...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿Hi,
>
>
> Sorry if this has been asked before and/or is very obvious but I couldn't 
> find anything about it.
>
> I reached 4 A with M and without classifying particles. I'd like to try to 
> sift out the worst particles in Relion5 (or in M, but I don't think that's 
> a feature?) to see if the resolution can improve further.
>
> Is there an easy way to export all particle coordinates and poses in a 
> format that Relion5 can ingest?
>
> Best,
> Mart
>
